query: CHP Ozgur Ozel Kilicdaroglu court ruling precautionary measure ihtiyati tedbir June 2026
answer:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the full research report.

---

## CHP Absolute Nullity Crisis: Court Ruling, Precautionary Measure (İhtiyati Tedbir), and the Özel–Kılıçdaroğlu Power Struggle (June 2026)

### 1. The Court Ruling

On **May 21, 2026**, the **36th Civil Chamber of the Ankara Regional Court of Justice (Ankara Bölge Adliye Mahkemesi / BAM)** issued a unanimous ruling declaring **"absolute nullity" (mutlak butlan)** of:

- CHP's **38th Ordinary Congress** (held November 4–5, 2023), at which Özgür Özel defeated Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu 812 to 536 delegate votes
- CHP's **21st Extraordinary Congress**

The ruling means these congresses are treated as if they **never legally occurred**.

### 2. The Precautionary Measure (İhtiyati Tedbir)

The court simultaneously issued an **interim injunction (ihtiyati tedbir)** that:

- **Suspended** chairman Özgür Özel and all party organs elected since the 2023 congress
- **Reinstated** former chairman Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu along with pre-2023 party organs on an interim basis
- This injunction remains in effect pending final judicial resolution

**Physical enforcement:** On **May 24, 2026**, Turkish police **stormed CHP headquarters** with tear gas and rubber bullets to physically evict Özel's administration and hand control to the Kılıçdaroğlu camp.

### 3. Factional Dynamics

| Dimension | **Özel Camp** | **Kılıçdaroğlu Camp** |
|---|---|---|
| **MP support** | ~111 of 138 (~80%) | ~27 of 138 |
| **Headquarters control** | No (evicted) | Yes (court-backed) |
| **Meeting venue** | Parliament | CHP headquarters |
| **Strategy** | Force extraordinary congress | Hold own congress process (autumn) |

### 4. Key Timeline of Events

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| **Nov 4–5, 2023** | Özel defeats Kılıçdaroğlu at 38th CHP Congress (812 vs 536 votes) |
| **Mar 31, 2024** | CHP wins landmark local elections (37.7% vote share, 35 provincial capitals) |
| **Mar 2025** | Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu arrested |
| **Apr 1, 2026** | CHP Deputy Group Chairman states "Plan B is ready" ahead of court ruling |
| **May 21, 2026** | Ankara BAM issues absolute nullity ruling and interim injunction; stock market drops 6%; $6–8B in state bank FX interventions |
| **May 24, 2026** | Police storm CHP headquarters with tear gas and rubber bullets |
| **May 27, 2026** | Özel rules out founding a new party: "We will stand by Atatürk's party and fight" |
| **Jun 1, 2026** | Özel camp begins collecting delegate signatures for extraordinary congress |
| **Jun 2, 2026** | Signature threshold exceeded (700+ vs ~550 needed) |
| **Jun 9, 2026** | Rival factions hold parallel meetings — Özel at Parliament, Kılıçdaroğlu at HQ |
| **Jun 10, 2026** | Kılıçdaroğlu camp refers 9 Özel-aligned members to High Disciplinary Board |
| **Jun 11, 2026** | 28 Özel-loyalist Party Assembly members resign (out of 57), dropping below 2/3 quorum → triggers mandatory extraordinary congress within 45 days |
| **Jun 11, 2026** | Kılıçdaroğlu launches own formal congress process for autumn |
| **Jun 12, 2026** | 111 CHP MPs declare call for extraordinary congress on July 12 |
| **Jun 16, 2026** | ~900 delegate signatures collected; 15-day collection period ends |
| **Jun 17–18, 2026** | Signatures to be formally submitted to CHP headquarters |

### 5. Legal Arguments on Both Sides

- **Özel camp:** The interim injunction is no barrier to a *new* congress — this constitutes a "new founding process." Prof. Ersan Şen supports this, arguing current management cannot prevent delegate-initiated congress.
- **Kılıçdaroğlu camp:** Party spokesman Müslüm Sarı states it is "not possible to convene the congress" under current circumstances — the interim injunction makes any congress legally impossible until the annulment ruling becomes final.
- **If Kılıçdaroğlu refuses to issue convening notice** (expected): Özel camp would apply to a **Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi** (Civil Court of Peace), which could appoint a *çağrı heyeti* (convening committee). Alternatively, delegates may self-issue a notice under CHP Statute Article 48.

### 6. Congress Target Dates

Two dates have been discussed:
1. **July 12, 2026** — declared by 111 MPs, aligned with a July 20 election deadline
2. **September 21, 2026** — mentioned in Habertürk reporting, possibly the actual operative target

### 7. Public Opinion

A **MetroPOLL survey** (May 2026) found:
- **62.1%** of all Turks opposed the court reinstatement of Kılıçdaroğlu
- **74.5%** of CHP voters opposed it
- **54.4%** of AKP (ruling party) voters also opposed the move

### 8. Economic Impact

- **6% stock market decline** on May 21, 2026
- **$6–8 billion** in state bank foreign exchange interventions to defend the Turkish lira

### 9. International Reaction

The EU, Socialist International, PES (Party of European Socialists), Germany, and Israel all criticized the ruling as undermining democracy.

### 10. Broader Political Context

The crisis is widely viewed as part of a broader AKP/Erdoğan strategy to weaken the Turkish opposition through the judiciary, following:
- The arrest of Istanbul Mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu in March 2025
- Detention of dozens of CHP mayors and hundreds of party officials since 2025
- Analysts suggest the crisis could trigger **snap elections as early as autumn 2026**

### 11. Historical Precedent (MHP 2016)

The closest precedent is the **MHP Extraordinary Congress crisis of 2016**, where dissidents collected signatures for a congress initially planned for May 15, 2016. Judiciary conflicts between different courts delayed proceedings significantly — the process took approximately 1–3 months from signature collection to actual congress.

key_facts: On May 21, 2026, the 36th Civil Chamber of the Ankara Regional Court of Justice (Ankara BAM) issued a unanimous 'absolute nullity' (mutlak butlan) ruling invalidating CHP's 38th Ordinary Congress (Nov 2023) and 21st Extraordinary Congress
The court simultaneously issued an interim injunction (ihtiyati tedbir) suspending Özgür Özel and reinstating Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu as CHP chairman, along with pre-2023 party organs
On May 24, 2026, Turkish police stormed CHP headquarters with tear gas and rubber bullets to evict Özel's administration
On May 27, 2026, Özel explicitly ruled out founding a new party: 'We have no intention of founding a new party. We will stand by Atatürk's party and fight'
Özel retains support of ~111 of 138 CHP MPs (~80%); Kılıçdaroğlu has ~27 MPs
On June 11, 2026, 28 Özel-loyalist Party Assembly members resigned (out of 57), dropping below 2/3 quorum, triggering mandatory extraordinary congress within 45 days
Kılıçdaroğlu launched his own formal congress process on June 11, 2026, targeting autumn
On June 10, Kılıçdaroğlu camp referred 9 Özel-aligned members to the High Disciplinary Board for potential expulsion
~900 delegate signatures collected by June 16 (well above ~550 threshold); to be submitted to CHP headquarters June 17–18
Two congress target dates discussed: July 12 (declared by 111 MPs, aligned with July 20 election deadline) and September 21 (mentioned in Habertürk)
MetroPOLL survey (May 2026): 62.1% of all Turks and 74.5% of CHP voters oppose the court reinstatement of Kılıçdaroğlu
The ruling triggered a 6% stock market drop and $6–8 billion in state bank FX interventions on May 21
EU, Socialist International, PES, Germany, and Israel criticized the ruling as undermining democracy
The crisis is widely viewed as part of a broader AKP/Erdoğan strategy to weaken the opposition through the judiciary, following İmamoğlu's imprisonment in March 2025
Legal dispute: Özel camp argues interim injunction is no barrier to a new congress ('new founding process'); Kılıçdaroğlu camp says congress is impossible until ruling is final
If Kılıçdaroğlu refuses convening notice, Özel camp would apply to Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Court of Peace) or delegates may self-issue under CHP Statute Article 48
Historical precedent: MHP 2016 extraordinary congress crisis took approximately 1–3 months from signature collection to actual congress
